import re

def test_token_expiry_uses_timedelta_string_format_and_validator__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'token_expiry' in source, 'Expected AuthenticatorEmailStage to define a token_expiry field.'

def test_token_expiry_uses_timedelta_string_format_and_validator__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'models.TextField' in source, "Expected token_expiry to be a TextField to allow timedelta string format (e.g. 'minutes=30') instead of an IntegerField."

def test_token_expiry_uses_timedelta_string_format_and_validator__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'timedelta_string_validator' in source, 'Expected token_expiry to use timedelta_string_validator for consistent duration parsing.'

def test_token_expiry_uses_timedelta_string_format_and_validator__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = re.compile('token_expiry\\s*=\\s*models\\.TextField\\(\\s*(?:.|\\n)*?default\\s*=\\s*[\\"\']minutes=30[\\"\']\\s*,(?:.|\\n)*?validators\\s*=\\s*\\[\\s*timedelta_string_validator\\s*\\]\\s*,', re.MULTILINE)
    assert pattern.search(source), "Expected token_expiry to be declared as models.TextField(default='minutes=30', validators=[timedelta_string_validator], ...) to use the standard timedelta string format."

def test_token_expiry_uses_timedelta_string_format_and_validator__a5():
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = re.compile('token_expiry\\s*=\\s*models\\.TextField\\(\\s*(?:.|\\n)*?default\\s*=\\s*[\\"\']minutes=30[\\"\']\\s*,(?:.|\\n)*?validators\\s*=\\s*\\[\\s*timedelta_string_validator\\s*\\]\\s*,', re.MULTILINE)
    assert 'token_expiry = models.IntegerField' not in source, 'token_expiry should no longer be an IntegerField (minutes-only); it should use a TextField with timedelta_string_validator.'

@user1:
We usually put those at the top of the function
results.test_code:
import re


def test_send_lazy_import_is_at_top_of_function_body():
    source = open("/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py", "r", encoding="utf-8").read()

    # Extract the body of `def send(self, device: "EmailDevice"):` up to the next method definition.
    m = re.search(
        r"\n\s*def\s+send\s*\(\s*self\s*,\s*device:\s*\"EmailDevice\"\s*\)\s*:\s*\n(?P<body>.*?)(?=\n\s*def\s+__str__\s*\(|\n\s*class\s+Meta\s*:)",
        source,
        flags=re.DOTALL,
    )
    assert m, "Expected to find AuthenticatorEmailStage.send() method in models.py"
    body = m.group("body")

    # Find first meaningful (non-empty, non-comment) statement in the function body.
    first_stmt = None
    for line in body.splitlines():
        stripped = line.strip()
        if not stripped:
            continue
        if stripped.startswith("#"):
            continue
        first_stmt = stripped
        break

    assert first_stmt is not None, "Expected AuthenticatorEmailStage.send() to have a non-empty body"

    assert (
        first_stmt.startswith("from authentik.stages.email.tasks import send_mails")
    ), (
        "AuthenticatorEmailStage.send() should place the lazy import at the top of the function body "
        "(first non-comment statement), per review comment. "
        f"Found first statement: {first_stmt!r}"
    )

import re

def test_authenticator_email_stage_removes_unused_logger__a1():
    """
    Review comment: "Never used" referring to LOGGER = get_logger() and its import.
    Ensure authentik/stages/authenticator_email/stage.py no longer defines/uses LOGGER.
    This should fail on the old code (LOGGER present) and pass on the new code (removed).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/stage.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'from structlog.stdlib import get_logger' not in source, 'stage.py should not import get_logger from structlog.stdlib (it was unused).'

def test_authenticator_email_stage_removes_unused_logger__a2():
    """
    Review comment: "Never used" referring to LOGGER = get_logger() and its import.
    Ensure authentik/stages/authenticator_email/stage.py no longer defines/uses LOGGER.
    This should fail on the old code (LOGGER present) and pass on the new code (removed).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/stage.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('^\\s*LOGGER\\s*=\\s*get_logger\\(\\)\\s*$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE) is None, 'stage.py should not define LOGGER = get_logger(); LOGGER was never used.'

import re


def test_authenticator_email_models_no_longer_defines_get_template_choices():
    """
    Review comment requests refactor of duplicated template-choice logic.
    Observable change: authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py should no longer
    define its own get_template_choices() helper (it was removed in the 'after' code).
    """
    source = open("/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py", "r", encoding="utf-8").read()

    has_func = re.search(r"(?m)^\s*def\s+get_template_choices\s*\(", source) is not None

    assert (
        not has_func
    ), "models.py should not define get_template_choices(); duplicated template-choice logic should be refactored out (function removed)."

# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level

import re

def test_email_subject_default_is_end_user_friendly_sign_in_code__a1():
    """
    Review requested changing the default email subject to a more end-user friendly
    value and specifically avoiding "OTP" wording. Since Django isn't available
    in this environment, assert via source inspection.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'subject = models.TextField(default="authentik Sign-in code")' in source, 'Expected AuthenticatorEmailStage.subject to default to "authentik Sign-in code" (end-user friendly), but it was not found in /workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py.'

def test_email_subject_default_is_end_user_friendly_sign_in_code__a2():
    """
    Review requested changing the default email subject to a more end-user friendly
    value and specifically avoiding "OTP" wording. Since Django isn't available
    in this environment, assert via source inspection.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    subject_default_match = re.search('subject\\s*=\\s*models\\.TextField\\(\\s*default\\s*=\\s*([\'\\"])(?P<val>.+?)\\1', source)
    assert subject_default_match, 'Could not locate a models.TextField default for subject in source.'

def test_email_subject_default_is_end_user_friendly_sign_in_code__a3():
    """
    Review requested changing the default email subject to a more end-user friendly
    value and specifically avoiding "OTP" wording. Since Django isn't available
    in this environment, assert via source inspection.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/authentik/stages/authenticator_email/models.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    subject_default_match = re.search('subject\\s*=\\s*models\\.TextField\\(\\s*default\\s*=\\s*([\'\\"])(?P<val>.+?)\\1', source)
    assert 'OTP' not in subject_default_match.group('val'), 'Subject default should not contain "OTP" to avoid confusing end users.'
